title: Razorpay Duplicate Payment ID
description: Razorpay's reconciliation team gets a list of payment IDs (as integers) processed in a day. They want to flag if any payment ID appears more than once because that signals a duplicate charge bug. Given an array `ids`, return `true` if at least one ID repeats, otherwise `false`.
hints: Set ka use kar — agar add karte waqt already present hai toh duplicate mil gaya.
Ek single pass me hi answer mil jayega.
Empty array nahi aayega per constraints, but single element ka case sochna.

title: Swiggy Order Pair Hitting Target
description: Swiggy is running a 'pair-and-save' promo: if any two orders in your cart sum exactly to the campaign target, you get a discount. Given `prices` (the cart) and `target` (the magic sum), return the indices of the two orders that match. Return them sorted ascending. Exactly one valid pair exists; you cannot pick the same index twice.
hints: Har element ke liye complement chahiye = target - current.
Hash map me value→index store kar, ek pass me check kar.
Same index twice mat use kar — map me current daalne se PEHLE complement check kar.

title: Flipkart Missing SKU Number
description: Flipkart's warehouse expects SKU numbers from 0 to n in every shipment crate. Today's crate has only n SKUs (one is missing). Given the array `skus` of length n containing distinct integers from the range [0, n], return the missing SKU.

examples.explanation: Range is 0..3 with n=3, missing is 2.

hints: Total sum of 0..n = n*(n+1)/2 — array sum se subtract kar de.
Ya hash set me daal aur 0..n iterate karke missing dhoondh.
n = length of array, range 0..n inclusive — total n+1 numbers but array has n only.

title: Zomato Restaurant Name Anagrams
description: Zomato's data team noticed many restaurant names are anagrams of each other (e.g., 'biryani' and 'aibinry'). Group them so the analytics dashboard can show clusters. Given `names` array of lowercase strings, return groups of anagrams. Each group should be sorted lexicographically internally, and the groups themselves should be sorted by their first element.

examples.explanation: Three groups, sorted internally and by first element.

hints: Anagrams ka sorted form same hota hai — use that as map key.
Map<string, string[]> bana, fir values nikaal le.
Output sort karna mat bhool — internal aur outer dono.

title: Paytm Most Frequent Transaction Amount
description: Paytm wants to know the single most common transaction amount in a list (the mode). Given `amounts`, return the value that appears most often. If there's a tie, return the smallest such value.

examples.explanation: Tie between 5 and 7; pick the smaller.

hints: Frequency count — Map ya plain object.
Iterate map: track best count + smallest value among ties.
Empty array nahi aayega; single element ka direct return sochna.

title: PhonePe Top K Merchants
description: PhonePe wants the top `k` merchants by transaction count. Given `merchants` (array of merchant ID integers, possibly repeating) and `k`, return the `k` IDs with the highest counts, sorted by count descending; if two merchants tie on count, the smaller ID comes first.

examples.explanation: Both tied at 2; smaller ID 4 first.

hints: Frequency count nikaal, fir entries ko sort kar count desc, id asc.
Slice top k.
k <= distinct count guaranteed hai per constraints.

title: Amazon Warehouse Common Inventory
description: Two Amazon warehouses share a fulfillment center. Given two arrays `wA` and `wB` of product IDs (with possible duplicates), return the unique IDs that appear in both warehouses, sorted ascending.
hints: Set ka use — wA ko set me daal, wB iterate karke common nikaal.
Result ko ek aur Set me daal duplicates avoid karne ke liye.
Last me sort ascending.

title: Razorpay Subarray Sum Equals Target
description: Razorpay's audit team scans contiguous chunks of daily settlement amounts (which can be negative on refunds) looking for any window whose total exactly equals a target. Given `settlements` and `target`, return how many contiguous subarrays sum to `target`.
hints: Prefix sum sochkar dekh — sum(i..j) = prefix[j] - prefix[i-1].
Map se count karo har prefix sum ko, fir (current - target) ka count add.
Initial map me {0: 1} daalna mat bhool — prefix that exactly equals target.

title: Swiggy Delivery Equilibrium Index
description: Swiggy plots the per-hour delivery counts for the day as an array. They want the leftmost hour `i` such that the total deliveries strictly before `i` equals the total strictly after `i`. Return that index, or `-1` if no such index exists.
hints: Total sum nikaal. Iterate karte waqt left running sum maintain kar.
At index i: right = total - left - arr[i]. If left === right, return i.
Single element: left=0, right=0 → index 0 is equilibrium.

title: Flipkart Reorder Detection
description: Flipkart maintains a `currentStock` array (length n, distinct integers). They also have a `targetStock` array — the same set of values but possibly in a different order. Determine whether `currentStock` is a permutation of `targetStock` (i.e., contains exactly the same multiset of items). Return `true` or `false`.
hints: Length compare karna pehla check.
Frequency map of one, decrement using other; agar koi negative ya missing → false.
Note: not stated distinct → handle duplicates correctly.

title: CRED Longest Consecutive Reward Streak
description: CRED logs reward levels reached by users as integers. Find the length of the longest run of consecutive integer levels present in the unsorted log. The run can start anywhere; gaps break the streak.
hints: Set me sab daal de.
Sirf streak ke 'starting point' se hi forward chal — agar n-1 set me hai, n start nahi hai.
Empty array ka case: return 0.

title: Meesho Product Price Difference Pairs
description: Meesho is bundling product pairs whose price difference is exactly `d`. Given an array `prices` of distinct positive integers and a non-negative integer `d`, return the count of unique unordered pairs `(a, b)` with `a < b` and `b - a == d`.

examples.explanation: All distinct, d=0 means no pair.

hints: Set bana de prices ka.
Har price p ke liye check kar agar (p + d) bhi present hai.
d = 0 special: distinct array me koi pair nahi banta.

title: Atlassian Sparse Matrix Equality
description: Atlassian's collaboration tool stores giant sparse matrices (mostly zero). Two matrices are given as 2D arrays `A` and `B` of identical dimensions. Decide if they are equal by hashing only their non-zero cells (so the comparison stays cheap on sparse data). Return `true` if all non-zero cells match in position and value, else `false`.
hints: Map<string,number> bana 'r,c' → value for non-zero cells of A.
B iterate kar: agar cell non-zero hai toh A me bhi same value chahiye, aur Map se hata.
End me Map empty hona chahiye.

title: Postman Rolling Unique API Calls
description: Postman's analytics dashboard shows a stream of API endpoint IDs hit per second. For every window of size `k`, return the count of distinct endpoints in that window. Output is an array of length `n - k + 1`. If `k > n`, return `[]`.
hints: Sliding window + frequency map.
Add element entering, decrement (and possibly delete) element leaving.
Map size = distinct count at any moment.

title: Uber Fare Bucket Modes
description: Uber bucketizes fares into buckets of width `w` (e.g., w=100 → buckets 0-99, 100-199, …). Given a `fares` array and bucket width `w` (positive), return the bucket id (the floor-division integer) that contains the most fares. Tie-break: return the smallest bucket id.

examples.explanation: Bucket 0: {50,99} count=2, bucket 1: {100} count=1, bucket 2: {250,270} count=2, bucket 3: {320} count=1. Tie at count 2 between buckets 0 and 2; return smaller, 0.

hints: Bucket id = Math.floor(fare / w).
Frequency map of bucket ids.
Tie break: smaller id.

title: Isomorphic Product Tags
description: A startup uses lowercase tag strings of equal length. Two tags are 'isomorphic' if you can rename characters in tag A (a consistent 1-to-1 mapping, no two source chars mapping to the same target) to obtain tag B. Given two strings `a` and `b`, return `true` if isomorphic, else `false`.

examples.explanation: o would map to both a and r.

hints: Do maps maintain karne padenge — a→b aur b→a.
Conflict detect karna hoga.
Equal length precondition hai per constraints.

title: PhonePe Net-Zero Day Spans
description: PhonePe records daily net cash flow (positive = inflow, negative = outflow). Find the LENGTH of the longest contiguous span of days where the net total is exactly zero. If none exists, return 0.
hints: Prefix sum first occurrence index ka map rakh.
Same prefix sum repeat hua → bich ka subarray sum zero.
Initial: prefix 0 at index -1.

title: Razorpay Largest Zero-Sum Submatrix
description: Razorpay's risk team scans an `R x C` grid where each cell is a signed integer (positive credits, negative debits). Find the AREA (cells × cells = R*C cells inside) of the largest contiguous rectangular submatrix whose elements sum to zero. If no zero-sum submatrix exists, return 0.
hints: 2D problem ko fix karke 1D banaya ja sakta hai — top row aur bottom row fix kar.
Column-wise compressed sum array banao, fir 1D longest-zero-subarray solve kar — but 'area' ke liye width track karna padega.
Best area = (bottom - top + 1) * (rightCol - leftCol + 1).

title: Amazon Check-in Anagram Windows
description: Amazon's barcode scanner streams a string `s` of lowercase letters and a pattern `p`. Return the count of substrings of `s` of length `|p|` that are anagrams of `p`.
hints: Sliding window of size |p| with frequency arrays of length 26.
Compare arrays — agar match toh +1.
Smart: track 'matches' counter to avoid full compare each step.

title: Swiggy Minimum Combo Window
description: Swiggy's combo offer requires the customer cart to contain ALL items from a target list (with multiplicity). Given `cart` (string of lowercase letters where each letter is an item) and `combo` (also a string), return the SHORTEST contiguous slice of `cart` that contains every item in `combo` (with multiplicity). If no such slice exists, return the empty string. Among ties, return the one with the smallest starting index.
hints: Sliding window: expand right until 'have' covers 'need', then shrink from left.
Counters: need-map (combo freq), have-map running, plus 'matched' integer for required positions filled.
Track best (minLen, startIdx).

title: Distinct Pairs With Sum Below K
description: Given an integer array `nums` and integer `k`, count the number of UNORDERED pairs `(a, b)` with `a < b` (by value, distinct values) such that `a + b < k`. Each unordered pair of distinct values is counted at most once, regardless of how many copies appear in `nums`.
hints: Set me daal de duplicates remove karne ke liye, fir array banao aur sort kar.
Two-pointer ya har element ke liye binary search.
n distinct ho toh worst O(n log n).

title: Microsoft Stable Frequency Sort
description: Sort the array `nums` so that elements with HIGHER frequency come first. If two values have the same frequency, the SMALLER value comes first. Within a value-group, repetitions are output consecutively.

examples.explanation: 1 and 2 tie at count 2; smaller (1) first.

hints: Frequency map bana.
Sort by (-count, value).
Re-emit each value count times in the sorted order.

title: Razorpay Group Settlements by Account
description: Razorpay receives an array of `[accountId, amount]` settlement records. Aggregate the total amount per account. Return an array of `[accountId, total]` sorted by accountId ascending.
hints: Map<accountId, totalAmount> bana.
Iterate records, accumulate.
End me entries → sort ascending by id.

title: Amazon Equal Approvals and Rejections Span
description: Amazon's review pipeline emits a binary log: `1` for approval, `0` for rejection. Find the LENGTH of the longest contiguous span containing an equal number of `0`s and `1`s.
hints: Treat 0 as -1, 1 as +1; problem reduces to longest zero-sum subarray.
Prefix sum + first-seen index map.
Initial: prefix 0 at index -1.

title: Google Shortest Substring With K Distinct
description: Given a lowercase string `s` and integer `k`, return the LENGTH of the shortest substring of `s` that contains exactly `k` distinct characters. If no such substring exists, return 0. (k=0 trivially has a 0-length valid substring; return 0 in that case.)
hints: k=0 → 0. k > distinct(s) → 0.
Sliding window with frequency map: expand, then shrink while distinct >= k tracking length.
Trick: shrink agressively when distinct === k or > k.
